name: Rhubarb-Strawberry-Jam
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the caps from strawberries. Crush berries one layer at a time. Trim (do not peel) rhubarb and thinly slice or chop stalks. Add water. Cover and simmer for 2 minutes, or until soft. Stir in prepared strawberries.
2. Measure 3 cups of prepared fruit. If it measures slightly less, add water. Place the measured fruit in a 6- or 8-quart saucepan.
3. Measure sugar exactly and set aside. Open liquid pectin and place the pouch upright in a cup.
4. Stir sugar into the prepared fruit. The saucepan must be no more than one-third full to allow for a full rolling boil.
5. Bring mixture to a full rolling boil over high heat. Boil hard 1 minute, stirring constantly. Remove from heat.
6. Stir pectin in at once. Quickly skim off foam with a large metal spoon. Immediately ladle into hot jars, leaving 1/4-inch space at the top. Wipe jar rims and threads clean with a damp cloth.
7. Cover jars with hot canning lids. Screw bands on firmly.
8. Place jars in a boiling water bath, carefully setting jars on a rack in the canner of boiling water. Cover canner and return water to a boil; boil 5 minutes.
9. Remove jars from canner and let cool. Check seals and store in a cool, dry place.
